seoTitle: Port Macquarie vs Port Stephens — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Macquarie and Port Stephens. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Macquarie vs Port Stephens: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Macquarie and Port Stephens.
heading: Port Macquarie vs Port Stephens — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Macquarie, New South Wales and Port Stephens, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Macquarie has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Port Stephens (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Macquarie or Port Stephens safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Macquarie has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Port Stephens scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Macquarie scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Port Stephens scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Macquarie or Port Stephens?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Macquarie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
